mongodb-1.subDescription: Welcome to the modern storage unit. Learn how to store folders (Documents) of any shape, use smart tags (ObjectIds), and perform basic searches.

phases.title: Touring the Facility: Databases & Collections
theory: MongoDB vs SQL: Flexible Bins vs Fixed Cabinets
The Storage Building (Database) and Storage Units (Collections)
Flexible Folders (Documents): Why any shape works
Digital Tags (ObjectIds): The auto-generated tracking number
BSON: The language the storage robot speaks
practicals: Create your first 'Franchise' building (Database)
Add a folder (Document) with a few items (Fields) to a bin

phases.title: Adding & Finding Folders: CRUD Basics
theory: insertOne & insertMany: Dropping off new shipments
The Search Robot (find): Looking for specific items
Projection: Asking the robot to only show certain items inside the folder
Sorting & Pagination: Arranging folders by date or skipping to the next aisle
practicals: Store 5 unique product folders in the 'Inventory' unit
Find all folders where 'Price' is greater than 100

mongodb-2.subDescription: Upgrade your storage efficiency. Master powerful search operators, the Barcode System (Indexing), and how to link folders together.

phases.title: Search Power-ups: Advanced Operators
theory: Comparison Operators ($gt, $lt): The 'Range Finder' tools
Logical Operators ($and, $or): Combining search rules
Array Operators ($in, $push): Handling folders that contain lists
Regex: Searching for folders using partial names or patterns
practicals: Update a folder to add a new 'Label' using $push
Find users who have 'Reading' OR 'Coding' in their hobby list

phases.title: The Barcode System: Indexing
theory: What is Indexing? The back-of-book index for your storage unit
Single vs Compound Indexes: Indexing by one label vs multiple
The Trade-off: Faster searching vs slower storage speed
Explain Mode: Checking if the robot is scanning every folder or using the index
practicals: Create a barcode (Index) for 'Email' to speed up staff login
Use 'Explain' to prove the robot is working faster with the barcode

phases.title: Facility Layout: Data Modeling
theory: Embedding: Keeping the receipt INSIDE the folder (Fast access)
Referencing: Keeping receipts in a separate bin (Saves space)
One-to-Many vs Many-to-Many: Deciding the best layout for your goods
Schema Validation: Setting rules for what can enter the facility
practicals: Design a 'Blog' layout: Should comments stay inside the post folder?
Set up a rule (Validator) that requires an 'Email' label for all new staff

mongodb-3.subDescription: Build an automated factory. Learn to use the Conveyor Belt (Aggregation) and the Inventory App (Mongoose) to manage everything.

phases.title: The Conveyor Belt: Aggregation Framework
theory: Aggregation Pipeline: A series of stations on a conveyor belt
$match & $sort: The first stations (Filter & Order)
$group & $sum: The calculation stations (Counting totals)
$lookup: Grabbing a matching folder from a different bin as it passes by
practicals: Build a pipeline to calculate total sales 'By Category'
Use $lookup to bring 'Customer Names' into the 'Orders' belt

phases.title: The Inventory App: Mongoose ODM
theory: What is Mongoose? The digital app for your facility
Schemas & Models: The app's rules and blueprints
Middleware (Hooks): Actions that happen 'Pre-storage' or 'Post-storage'
Virtuals: Labels that only exist in the app, not on the physical folder
practicals: Set up a Mongoose blueprint for a 'Product'
Automate a 'Password Hashing' task before saving a staff folder

phases.title: Global Scaling: Replication & Sharding
theory: Replication: Keeping copies of your storage facility for safety
Sharding: Breaking a massive facility into multiple smaller ones
Transactions: The 'All-or-Nothing' storage contract
Security: Role-based access for different facility staff
practicals: Mock a 'Money Transfer' using Transactions
List the steps to secure a facility with an Admin badge

mongodb-questions.subDescription: 50+ common questions. Master the 'Flexible Storage' analogy to impress auditors (Interviewers).

phases.title: Ground Level: Core Concepts
theory: MongoDB vs SQL (Flexible Bins vs Rigid Cabinets)
When to Embed vs Reference (The Receipt Analogy)
What is BSON? (The Robot's internal language)
Understanding ObjectIds (The Digital Tracking Tag)
practicals: Explain Aggregation using the 'Conveyor Belt' metaphor
Differentiate CRUD from SQL commands using the 'Folder' analogy

phases.title: Top Floor: Advanced Performance
theory: Index Prefix Rule (The 'Matching Label' rule)
Covered Queries (When the robot doesn't even open the folder)
Sharding Strategies (How to split a global facility)
Atomic Operations (When a single folder update is enough)
practicals: Simulate a 'Slow Pipeline' and fix it with a $match station at the start
Design an index strategy for a 'High Traffic' storage unit
